sentences.sentence: According to archaeological evidence, at least 5,000 years ago, and long before the advent of the Roman Empire, the Babylonians began to measure time, introducing calendars to co-ordinate communal activities, to plan the shipment of goods and, in particular, to regulate planting and harvesting.
sentences.translation: 根据考古证据，至少在5000年前、远早于罗马帝国出现之时，巴比伦人就开始计量时间，并制定日历来协调公共活动、规划货物运输，尤其是安排播种和收割。

structure.text: According to archaeological evidence
at least 5,000 years ago, and long before the advent of the Roman Empire
the Babylonians began to measure time
introducing calendars to co-ordinate communal activities, to plan the shipment of goods and, in particular, to regulate planting and harvesting

difficult_points: introducing 分词短语补充说明 began to measure time 后的结果和目的。
三个 to 不定式并列，分别说明日历的用途。

sentences.sentence: They based their calendars on three natural cycles: the solar day, marked by the successive periods of light and darkness as the earth rotates on its axis; the lunar month, following the phases of the moon as it orbits the earth; and the solar year, defined by the changing seasons that accompany our planet's revolution around the sun.
sentences.translation: 他们把日历建立在三个自然周期之上：太阳日，即地球绕轴旋转时光明与黑暗的连续周期；太阴月，即月球绕地球运行时月相的变化；以及太阳年，即由伴随地球绕太阳公转而变化的季节所定义的周期。

structure.text: They
based their calendars on three natural cycles
the solar day, marked by the successive periods of light and darkness as the earth rotates on its axis
the lunar month, following the phases of the moon as it orbits the earth
the solar year, defined by the changing seasons that accompany our planet's revolution around the sun
that accompany our planet's revolution around the sun

difficult_points: 冒号后列出三个 cycles，并用分号分隔。
marked、following、defined 是分词短语，分别补充说明三个周期。

sentences.sentence: Before the invention of artificial light, the moon had greater social impact.
sentences.translation: 在人造光发明之前，月亮的社会影响更大。

structure.text: Before the invention of artificial light
the moon
had greater social impact

difficult_points: Before the invention of... 是时间状语。
greater 是比较级，暗含与后来人造光时代的比较。

sentences.sentence: And, for those living near the equator in particular, its waxing and waning was more conspicuous than the passing of the seasons.
sentences.translation: 尤其对于生活在赤道附近的人来说，月亮的盈亏比季节的更替更加显眼。

structure.text: for those living near the equator in particular
living near the equator
its waxing and waning
was more conspicuous than the passing of the seasons

difficult_points: living near the equator 修饰 those。
waxing and waning 是名词化的‘盈亏’，than 引导比较对象。

sentences.sentence: Hence, the calendars that were developed at the lower latitudes were influenced more by the lunar cycle than by the solar year.
sentences.translation: 因此，在低纬度地区制定的日历受太阴月周期的影响比受太阳年的影响更大。

structure.text: Hence
the calendars that were developed at the lower latitudes
that were developed at the lower latitudes
were influenced more by the lunar cycle than by the solar year

difficult_points: that were developed... 修饰 calendars。
more by... than by... 构成比较结构，且主句是被动语态。

sentences.sentence: In more northern climes, however, where seasonal agriculture was practised, the solar year became more crucial.
sentences.translation: 然而，在实行季节性农业的北方地区，太阳年变得更加重要。

structure.text: In more northern climes
where seasonal agriculture was practised
the solar year became more crucial

difficult_points: where 从句修饰 northern climes。
was practised 是被动语态，表示农业活动被实行。

sentences.sentence: As the Roman Empire expanded northward, it organised its activity chart for the most part around the solar year.
sentences.translation: 随着罗马帝国向北扩张，它的活动安排大多围绕太阳年制定。

structure.text: As the Roman Empire expanded northward
it organised its activity chart for the most part around the solar year

difficult_points: As 引导时间或伴随变化从句。
for the most part 表示‘大部分情况下’。

sentences.sentence: Centuries before the Roman Empire, the Egyptians had formulated a municipal calendar having 12 months of 30 days, with five days added to approximate the solar year.
sentences.translation: 早在罗马帝国之前数百年，埃及人就制定了一种市政日历，包含12个月、每月30天，并增加5天来大致对应太阳年。

structure.text: Centuries before the Roman Empire
the Egyptians
had formulated
a municipal calendar having 12 months of 30 days
with five days added to approximate the solar year

difficult_points: had formulated 是过去完成时，表示早于另一过去时间。
having... 和 with... 都是补充说明日历构成的结构。

sentences.sentence: Each period of ten days was marked by the appearance of special groups of stars called decans.
sentences.translation: 每个十天的时期都以被称为‘十日星’的特殊星群出现为标志。

structure.text: Each period of ten days
was marked by
the appearance of special groups of stars called decans
called decans

difficult_points: was marked by 是被动结构，表示以某事为标志。
called decans 修饰 groups of stars。

sentences.sentence: At the rise of the star Sirius just before sunrise, which occurred around the all-important annual flooding of the Nile, 12 decans could be seen spanning the heavens.
sentences.translation: 在天狼星于日出前升起时——这发生在至关重要的尼罗河年度泛滥前后——可以看到12组十日星横跨天空。

structure.text: At the rise of the star Sirius just before sunrise
which occurred around the all-important annual flooding of the Nile
12 decans could be seen spanning the heavens
spanning the heavens

difficult_points: which 指代前面天狼星升起这一事件，而非单个名词。
could be seen spanning 使用 see + 宾语 + 现在分词结构。

sentences.sentence: The cosmic significance the Egyptians placed in the 12 decans led them to develop a system in which each interval of darkness (and later, each interval of daylight) was divided into a dozen equal parts.
sentences.translation: 埃及人赋予12组十日星的宇宙意义促使他们建立了一套系统，把每段黑暗时间（后来也包括每段白昼）分成12个等份。

structure.text: The cosmic significance the Egyptians placed in the 12 decans
the Egyptians placed in the 12 decans
led them to develop a system
in which each interval of darkness (and later, each interval of daylight) was divided into a dozen equal parts

difficult_points: the Egyptians placed... 是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰 significance。
lead sb to do 表示‘促使某人做某事’；in which 引导 system 的定语从句。

sentences.sentence: These periods became known as temporal hours because their duration varied according to the changing length of days and nights with the passing of the seasons.
sentences.translation: 这些时段被称为‘时辰’，因为它们的时长会随着季节流逝、昼夜长度变化而改变。

structure.text: These periods
became known as temporal hours
because their duration varied according to the changing length of days and nights with the passing of the seasons

difficult_points: become known as 表示‘被称为’。
because 从句说明时辰长度变化的原因。

sentences.sentence: Summer hours were long, winter ones short; only at the spring and autumn equinoxes were the hours of daylight and darkness equal.
sentences.translation: 夏季的时辰很长，冬季的很短；只有在春分和秋分时，白昼和黑夜的时长才相等。

structure.text: Summer hours were long, winter ones short
only at the spring and autumn equinoxes were the hours of daylight and darkness equal

difficult_points: winter ones 中 ones 代替 winter hours。
Only at 置于句首导致部分倒装，were 提前。

sentences.sentence: Temporal hours, which were first adopted by the Greeks and then the Romans, who disseminated them through Europe, remained in use for more than 2,500 years.
sentences.translation: 时辰制先被希腊人采用，随后被将其传播到欧洲的罗马人采用，并持续使用了2500多年。

structure.text: Temporal hours
which were first adopted by the Greeks and then the Romans
who disseminated them through Europe
remained in use for more than 2,500 years

difficult_points: which 修饰 Temporal hours；who 修饰 Romans。
were adopted 是被动语态，remained in use 表示持续使用。

sentences.sentence: In order to track temporal hours during the day, inventors created sundials, which indicate time by the length or direction of the sun's shadow.
sentences.translation: 为了记录白天的时辰，发明家创造了日晷，它通过太阳影子的长度或方向来显示时间。

structure.text: In order to track temporal hours during the day
inventors created sundials
which indicate time by the length or direction of the sun's shadow

difficult_points: In order to 引导目的不定式。
which 指代 sundials，by 短语说明显示时间的方式。

sentences.sentence: The sundial's counterpart, the water clock, was designed to measure temporal hours at night.
sentences.translation: 日晷的对应物水钟被设计用来测量夜间的时辰。

structure.text: The sundial's counterpart, the water clock
the water clock
was designed to measure temporal hours at night

difficult_points: the water clock 是 The sundial's counterpart 的同位语。
was designed to measure 是被动语态加目的不定式。

sentences.sentence: One of the first water clocks was a basin with a small hole near the bottom through which the water dripped out.
sentences.translation: 最早的水钟之一是一个盆，底部附近有一个小孔，水从孔中滴出。

structure.text: One of the first water clocks
was a basin with a small hole near the bottom
through which the water dripped out

difficult_points: One of... 表示‘……之一’。
through which 指水流出的通道，which 指 small hole。

sentences.sentence: The falling water level denoted the passing hour as it dipped below hour lines inscribed on the inner surface.
sentences.translation: 当水位下降到刻在内壁上的小时刻线以下时，下降的水位就表示一个时辰过去了。

structure.text: The falling water level
denoted the passing hour
as it dipped below hour lines inscribed on the inner surface
inscribed on the inner surface

difficult_points: as 引导时间从句，it 指 water level。
inscribed on the inner surface 修饰 hour lines。

sentences.sentence: Although these devices performed satisfactorily around the Mediterranean, they could not always be depended on in the cloudy and often freezing weather of northern Europe.
sentences.translation: 尽管这些设备在地中海周边运行良好，但在北欧多云且常常严寒的天气中，它们并不总是可靠。

structure.text: Although these devices performed satisfactorily around the Mediterranean
they could not always be depended on in the cloudy and often freezing weather of northern Europe

difficult_points: Although 引导让步从句。
could not be depended on 是被动短语，表示‘不能被依赖’。

sentences.sentence: The advent of the mechanical clock meant that although it could be adjusted to maintain temporal hours, it was naturally suited to keeping equal ones.
sentences.translation: 机械钟的出现意味着，尽管它可以调节以保持时辰制，但它天生更适合计量等长的时间。

structure.text: The advent of the mechanical clock
meant that although it could be adjusted to maintain temporal hours, it was naturally suited to keeping equal ones
although it could be adjusted to maintain temporal hours

difficult_points: meant 后接 that 从句；although 从句嵌在其中。
be suited to 后接动名词 keeping。

sentences.sentence: With these, however, arose the question of when to begin counting, and so, in the early 14th century, a number of systems evolved.
sentences.translation: 然而，随着这些钟的出现，何时开始计时的问题产生了；于是，14世纪初出现了许多不同的计时制度。

structure.text: With these
arose the question of when to begin counting
of when to begin counting
and so, in the early 14th century, a number of systems evolved

difficult_points: With these 置于句首，主句倒装为 arose the question。
when to begin counting 是 question 的同位内容。

sentences.sentence: The schemes that divided the day into 24 equal parts varied according to the start of the count: Italian hours began at sunset, Babylonian hours at sunrise, astronomical hours at midday and 'great clock' hours, used for some large public clocks in Germany, at midnight.
sentences.translation: 把一天分成24个等份的制度因计时起点不同而变化：意大利时辰从日落开始，巴比伦时辰从日出开始，天文时辰从正午开始，而德国一些大型公共钟使用的‘大钟时辰’从午夜开始。

structure.text: The schemes that divided the day into 24 equal parts
that divided the day into 24 equal parts
varied according to the start of the count
Italian hours began at sunset, Babylonian hours at sunrise, astronomical hours at midday and 'great clock' hours, used for some large public clocks in Germany, at midnight
used for some large public clocks in Germany

difficult_points: that divided... 修饰 schemes。
冒号后并列列出四种计时制度，后三项省略了 began。

sentences.sentence: Eventually these were superseded by 'small clock', or French, hours, which split the day into two 12-hour periods commencing at midnight.
sentences.translation: 最终，这些制度被‘小钟时辰’或法国时辰取代，后者把一天分成从午夜开始的两个12小时阶段。

structure.text: Eventually
these were superseded by 'small clock', or French, hours
which split the day into two 12-hour periods commencing at midnight
commencing at midnight

difficult_points: were superseded by 表示‘被……取代’。
which 修饰 French hours；commencing 修饰 periods。

sentences.sentence: The earliest recorded weight-driven mechanical clock was built in 1283 in Bedfordshire in England.
sentences.translation: 有记录的最早的重锤驱动机械钟于1283年在英格兰贝德福德郡制造。

structure.text: The earliest recorded weight-driven mechanical clock
was built
in 1283 in Bedfordshire in England

difficult_points: weight-driven 是复合形容词，表示由重锤驱动。
was built 是被动语态。

sentences.sentence: The revolutionary aspect of this new timekeeper was neither the descending weight that provided its motive force nor the gear wheels (which had been around for at least 1,300 years) that transferred the power; it was the part called the escapement.
sentences.translation: 这台新计时器的革命性之处既不是提供动力的下降重锤，也不是传递动力的齿轮（齿轮至少存在了1300年）；而是那个被称为擒纵机构的部件。

structure.text: The revolutionary aspect of this new timekeeper
was neither the descending weight that provided its motive force nor the gear wheels (which had been around for at least 1,300 years) that transferred the power
that provided its motive force
which had been around for at least 1,300 years
that transferred the power
it was the part called the escapement

difficult_points: neither...nor... 构成‘既不是……也不是……’。
多个 that/which 从句分别修饰 weight、gear wheels 和 gear wheels。

sentences.sentence: In the early 1400s came the invention of the coiled spring or fusee which maintained constant force to the gear wheels of the timekeeper despite the changing tension of its mainspring.
sentences.translation: 15世纪初，盘簧或链条式装置问世；尽管主发条张力变化，它仍能给计时器齿轮提供恒定动力。

structure.text: In the early 1400s came the invention of the coiled spring or fusee
which maintained constant force to the gear wheels of the timekeeper despite the changing tension of its mainspring

difficult_points: In the early 1400s 置于句首引起主谓倒装。
which 修饰 coiled spring or fusee；despite 引导让步介词短语。

sentences.sentence: By the 16th century, a pendulum clock had been devised, but the pendulum swung in a large arc and thus was not very efficient.
sentences.translation: 到16世纪，人们发明了摆钟，但摆锤摆动的弧度很大，因此效率不高。

structure.text: By the 16th century
a pendulum clock had been devised
but the pendulum swung in a large arc and thus was not very efficient

difficult_points: had been devised 是过去完成时被动语态。
thus 是结果副词，连接摆幅大与效率低。

sentences.sentence: To address this, a variation on the original escapement was invented in 1670, in England.
sentences.translation: 为了解决这一问题，1670年英格兰发明了原始擒纵机构的一种变体。

structure.text: To address this
a variation on the original escapement
was invented
in 1670, in England

difficult_points: To address this 表示目的，this 指摆钟效率不高的问题。
was invented 是被动语态。

sentences.sentence: It was called the anchor escapement, which was a lever-based device shaped like a ship's anchor.
sentences.translation: 它被称为锚式擒纵机构，是一种形状像船锚的杠杆装置。

structure.text: It was called the anchor escapement
which was a lever-based device shaped like a ship's anchor
shaped like a ship's anchor

difficult_points: which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 anchor escapement。
shaped like... 修饰 device。

sentences.sentence: The motion of a pendulum rocks this device so that it catches and then releases each tooth of the escape wheel, in turn allowing it to turn a precise amount.
sentences.translation: 摆锤的运动使这个装置来回摆动，从而依次卡住并释放擒纵轮的每个齿，使其转动精确的幅度。

structure.text: The motion of a pendulum rocks this device
so that it catches and then releases each tooth of the escape wheel
in turn allowing it to turn a precise amount

difficult_points: so that 引导结果从句；it 指 device。
allowing 分词短语表示随后产生的结果，it 指 escape wheel。

sentences.sentence: Unlike the original form used in early pendulum clocks, the anchor escapement permitted the pendulum to travel in a very small arc.
sentences.translation: 与早期摆钟使用的原始形式不同，锚式擒纵机构使摆锤能够以很小的弧度摆动。

structure.text: Unlike the original form used in early pendulum clocks
used in early pendulum clocks
the anchor escapement permitted the pendulum to travel in a very small arc

difficult_points: used in early pendulum clocks 修饰 original form。
permit someone to do 表示‘允许某人做某事’。

sentences.sentence: Moreover, this invention allowed the use of a long pendulum which could beat once a second and thus led to the development of a new floor-standing case design, which became known as the grandfather clock.
sentences.translation: 此外，这项发明允许使用每秒摆动一次的长摆锤，从而促成了一种新的落地钟箱体设计，这种设计后来被称为祖父钟。

structure.text: this invention allowed the use of a long pendulum
which could beat once a second
and thus led to the development of a new floor-standing case design
which became known as the grandfather clock

difficult_points: which could beat... 修饰 long pendulum；第二个 which 修饰 case design。
lead to 表示‘导致、促成’。

sentences.sentence: Today, highly accurate timekeeping instruments set the beat for most electronic devices.
sentences.translation: 如今，高度精确的计时仪器为大多数电子设备设定节拍。

structure.text: Today
highly accurate timekeeping instruments
set the beat for most electronic devices

difficult_points: set the beat for 是比喻表达，表示为设备提供节奏或基准。
highly accurate 修饰 timekeeping instruments。

sentences.sentence: Nearly all computers contain a quartz-crystal clock to regulate their operation.
sentences.translation: 几乎所有计算机都含有石英晶体钟来调节其运行。

structure.text: Nearly all computers
contain
a quartz-crystal clock
to regulate their operation

difficult_points: to regulate 是目的不定式，说明石英钟的用途。
their 指 nearly all computers。

sentences.sentence: Moreover, not only do time signals beamed down from Global Positioning System satellites calibrate the functions of precision navigation equipment, they do so as well for mobile phones, instant stock-trading systems and nationwide power-distribution grids.
sentences.translation: 此外，从全球定位系统卫星发射下来的时间信号不仅能校准精密导航设备的功能，也能校准手机、即时股票交易系统和全国电力分配网络。

structure.text: not only do time signals beamed down from Global Positioning System satellites calibrate the functions of precision navigation equipment
beamed down from Global Positioning System satellites
they do so as well for mobile phones, instant stock-trading systems and nationwide power-distribution grids

difficult_points: Not only 置于句首引起部分倒装，do 提前。
beamed down 修饰 time signals；do so 代替前面的 calibrate。

sentences.sentence: So integral have these time-based technologies become to day-to-day existence that our dependency on them is recognised only when they fail to work.
sentences.translation: 这些基于时间的技术已经成为日常生活不可或缺的一部分，以至于我们只有在它们失灵时才意识到自己对它们的依赖。

structure.text: So integral have these time-based technologies become to day-to-day existence
that our dependency on them is recognised only when they fail to work
only when they fail to work

difficult_points: So...that... 结构置于句首导致部分倒装：have technologies become。
only when 从句说明意识到依赖的条件，fail to work 表示失灵。
